build_system: CMake 3.22 with vendored third-party deps in third_party/. Static libraries per module. Link order matters (see CMakeLists.txt subdirectory order).
test_framework: doctest (header-only, vendored in third_party/doctest/include). Single test executable 'threatone_tests' that compiles all test sources. Tests are in tests/<module>/ directories.
verification_instructions: 1. cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF 2. cmake --build build (must compile cleanly) 3. cd build && ctest --output-on-failure (all tests pass)
setup_instructions: No setup needed - CMake 3.22, GCC 11.5 pre-installed, all deps vendored in third_party/
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network - no external downloads. All libraries must be vendored or implemented from scratch. No external ML libraries. Use standard C++20 only.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing patterns: namespace ThreatOne::<Module>, #pragma once headers, ModuleLogger per class, Result<T> for error handling, struct-based data types, doctest for testing.
key_patterns: 1. Each module: include/<module>/*.h (headers) + src/*.cpp (impl). 2. Interface classes prefixed with 'I' (IAIEngine, IAnalyticsEngine). 3. ModuleLogger from Core::Logger::instance().getModuleLogger(name). 4. Thread safety via std::mutex. 5. nlohmann_json for serialization. 6. Tests use TEST_CASE with CHECK/REQUIRE macros. 7. CMakeLists.txt lists all .cpp sources explicitly.
directory_structure: src/<module>/include/<module>/*.h for public headers, src/<module>/src/*.cpp for implementation. tests/<module>/test_*.cpp for tests. third_party/ has spdlog, nlohmann json, doctest, sqlite3 (all vendored header-only or stubs).
